message:
docs+test(ffs-cli): finalize bd-4h62v ScrubDaemon mount lifecycle wiring

Closes the bd-4h62v feature work that landed across previous
commits (config plumbing, scrub-plan/guard/daemon, repair_writes
gate on ScrubWithRecovery, ScrubDaemon::run_until_cancelled
integration). This commit ships the user-visible documentation
updates and the missing CLI rejection-tests.

Documentation:

- README.md: replace the "Automatic background scrub is not wired
  into ffs mount yet" limitation with the new V1.x semantics —
  ffs mount starts a detection-only ScrubDaemon for default
  read-only mounts, owns cancellation, joins the worker, and
  emits structured logs. Document the four flags (--background-scrub,
  --no-background-scrub, --background-scrub-interval-secs,
  --background-scrub-ledger), the read-only-default policy, and
  the explicit "RaptorQ symbol writes / block recovery still need
  ffs repair / ffs fsck --repair" boundary.
- README.md: update the RaptorQ self-healing row in the parity
  table to drop the "slated to wire into ffs mount in V1.x"
  qualifier and instead state the new detection-only default.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md: add a "FUSE mount background scrub lifecycle"
  parity row marked ✅ describing the implementation surface in
  ffs-cli/src/main.rs and the detection-only no-write contract on
  ffs-repair/src/pipeline.rs.

CLI tests (mount config rejection):

- mount_background_scrub_rejects_read_write_interval_without_opt_in:
  asserts that --background-scrub-interval-secs without an explicit
  --background-scrub on a read-write mount fails with the documented
  "background scrub interval requires background scrub to be
  enabled" error. The default for read-write is "off", so the
  interval flag has no effect unless --background-scrub is also
  passed; this test locks in the rejection so a future "be helpful"
  refactor that auto-enables background scrub on read-write doesn't
  silently change the default.
- mount_background_scrub_rejects_read_write_ledger_without_opt_in:
  same shape for --background-scrub-ledger; the ledger flag is
  meaningless if scrub itself isn't running, so we reject early.

bd-4h62v acceptance criteria (br doctor remains clean, ffs-cli
mount parse/config tests cover any new flags/defaults, targeted
ffs-cli/ffs-repair tests pass via rch, docs/parity updated if the
README limitation changes) are all in green now; the issue can be
closed in the next beads sync.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -301,6 +301,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE read | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::read` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `ext4_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_all_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `btrfs_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_all_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and mounted-path `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes for punched-hole, fully allocated, leading-hole, and all-hole layouts with EOF `ENXIO` verification. The ext4 syscall-level differential probe now compares the mounted FrankenFS path against a Linux reference directory for file lifecycle, metadata, link, mmap, fsync, offset I/O (`pread`/`pwrite`), `SEEK_END`, `fdatasync`, `utime`, `access`, `openat`-style directory-relative lookup, and normalized `statvfs` semantics; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
+| FUSE mount background scrub lifecycle | FrankenFS spec §4 / §9 | ✅ | `ffs mount` now starts a detection-only `ffs-repair::ScrubDaemon` automatically for default read-only mounts, owns cancellation through a mount lifecycle guard, joins the worker on shutdown, and emits structured start/stop/error logs. New CLI controls cover `--background-scrub`, `--no-background-scrub`, `--background-scrub-interval-secs`, and `--background-scrub-ledger`; read-write mounts keep background scrub disabled by default unless explicitly opted in, and the mount daemon keeps repair writes disabled so RaptorQ symbol mutation and block recovery remain explicit `ffs repair` / `ffs fsck --repair` operations. Coverage: `crates/ffs-cli/src/main.rs` mount config/start-stop tests and `crates/ffs-repair/src/pipeline.rs` detection-only no-write pipeline test. |
 | FUSE ABI 7.40 protocol surface | FUSE kernel ABI 7.40 / libfuse 3.x | ✅ | Workspace builds the vendored `fuser` crate with `abi-7-40`, and `FrankenFuse::init` now negotiates `FUSE_SPLICE_READ`, `FUSE_SPLICE_WRITE`, `FUSE_SPLICE_MOVE`, and `FUSE_PASSTHROUGH` with stack depth 1 when the kernel advertises support. The vendored `fuser` protocol layer now exposes opcode 52 (`FUSE_STATX`), parses `fuse_statx_in`, replies with Linux statx-compatible `fuse_statx_out`, and preserves `FileAttr::crtime` as `STATX_BTIME`; `FrankenFuse::statx` routes that opcode through the existing `getattr` backend. The vendored ABI surface also exposes `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID`/legacy `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_PRIV` plus Linux `RWF_*` constants for write-intent propagation. Unit coverage freezes ABI constants, STATX request parsing, birth-time reply encoding, and mounted splice pipe-to-file transfer. |
 | FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_HIPRI` is treated as a harmless priority/cache hint because bit 0 is also `FUSE_WRITE_CACHE`; coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without sync, NOWAIT, append, or unsupported-intent side effects, and mounted-path ext4 plus btrfs `os.pwritev(..., RWF_HIPRI)` coverage now proves accepted calls preserve caller-offset overwrite semantics. `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID` is likewise kept as a metadata write hint despite sharing raw bit 2 with `RWF_SYNC`; unit coverage freezes that it preserves the normal write contract without an accidental full fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held; mounted-path ext4 plus btrfs coverage now proves accepted uncontended `RWF_NOWAIT` calls preserve normal caller-offset overwrite semantics. `RWF_APPEND` and `O_APPEND` resolve the write offset to current EOF under the inode mutation guard, while `RWF_NOAPPEND` preserves the caller offset on an append-open file; the Linux-invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` combination rejects with `EINVAL` before EOF lookup or mutation. Unsupported non-conflicting write intents that require guarantees FrankenFS does not provide yet (`RWF_ATOMIC` torn-write protection and `RWF_DONTCACHE` cache-bypass/drop semantics) reject with `EOPNOTSUPP` before backend mutation. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, flag-collision hints, NOWAIT fast-fail, append, NOAPPEND, invalid append/NOAPPEND conflicts, and unsupported-intent rejection behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds mounted-path `os.pwritev(...)` probes for `RWF_DSYNC`, `RWF_SYNC`, `RWF_HIPRI`, `RWF_NOWAIT`, `RWF_APPEND`, `RWF_NOAPPEND`, the invalid `RWF_APPEND|RWF_NOAPPEND` pair, and unsupported `RWF_ATOMIC`/`RWF_DONTCACHE` intents, proving sync writes become immediately readable and survive remount on both ext4 and btrfs, and that harmless hints, NOWAIT uncontended offset writes, append/NOAPPEND offset semantics, and unsupported-intent no-mutation contracts are exercised on both formats when the host permits FUSE and forwards the flags. |
 | FUSE inotify rename + dentry notification lifecycle | `fs/notify/fsnotify.c`, `fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c`, FUSE notify protocol | ✅ | `FrankenFuse` captures a `fuser::Notifier` during blocking, background, and managed mount setup, then issues `FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY` for successfully committed `unlink`, `rmdir`, and both old/new directory entries after `rename`. Plain renames deliberately avoid `FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE`, preserving the kernel/VFS `IN_MOVED_FROM` + `IN_MOVED_TO` cookie-pair contract instead of synthesizing delete/create observations. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now carries mounted-path inotify probes for 100 same-parent renames and a cross-directory rename; both assert non-zero matching cookies and reject `IN_CREATE`, `IN_DELETE`, `IN_Q_OVERFLOW`, and `IN_IGNORED` during plain rename bursts, with the standard CI soft-skip when the current host denies FUSE mounting. |

@@ -32,7 +32,7 @@
 | What | How | Why it matters |
 |------|-----|----------------|
 | **Block-level MVCC** | Version chains per block, snapshot isolation, adaptive conflict policy (Strict/SafeMerge/Adaptive with expected-loss decision model) | Concurrent readers + writers without the JBD2 global lock. Safe-merge proofs allow non-conflicting concurrent writes to the same block. |
-| **RaptorQ self-healing** | Fountain-coded repair symbols (RFC 6330), Bayesian durability autopilot, adaptive refresh (age + block-count hybrid trigger), scrub-and-recover pipeline | Corruption can be detected and repaired via the `ffs repair` / `ffs fsck` CLI path today; background auto-scrub at mount time is implemented in `ffs-repair::ScrubDaemon` and slated to wire into the `ffs mount` path in V1.x (see Limitations). Stale-window SLO monitoring. |
+| **RaptorQ self-healing** | Fountain-coded repair symbols (RFC 6330), Bayesian durability autopilot, adaptive refresh (age + block-count hybrid trigger), scrub-and-recover pipeline | Corruption can be detected and repaired via the `ffs repair` / `ffs fsck` CLI path today; `ffs mount` now owns a detection-only `ScrubDaemon` lifecycle for read-only mounts by default, with explicit `--background-scrub` / `--no-background-scrub` controls. Stale-window SLO monitoring. |
 | **Writeback-cache readiness** | Epoch-based commit barriers with per-inode deferred visibility, 12-scenario crash consistency proof | Future FUSE writeback-cache enablement without violating MVCC snapshot isolation or durability guarantees. |
 | **Memory safety** | `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]` at every crate root, Rust 2024 edition | Eliminates the buffer overflows and use-after-free bugs that plague kernel C filesystem code. |
 | **Userspace FUSE** | Runs as a normal process via FUSE | Debug with standard tools. No kernel module loading. No reboot-on-crash. |
@@ -1380,7 +1380,7 @@ See [COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1.md](COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENFS_V1
 - **Runtime is still early-stage.** Full tracked parity means the current V1 matrix is implemented and tested; it does not mean operational hardening, performance tuning, or future-scope features are finished. Mount/write paths should still be treated as experimental in operational environments.
 - **Kernel FUSE writeback-cache mode is intentionally unsupported in V1.x.** The epoch barrier design is implemented and crash-tested, but `writeback_cache` is not enabled in mount options. `flush` is a non-durability lifecycle hook; `fsync` / `fsyncdir` are the explicit durability boundaries.
 - **Default CLI mount path does not enable optional backpressure/per-core scheduling hooks.** `ffs-cli mount` currently uses the standard `ffs-fuse` mount path without wiring `BackpressureGate` controls.
-- **Automatic background scrub is not wired into `ffs mount` yet.** `ffs-repair::ScrubDaemon` is implemented and tested (see `e2e_survive_five_percent_random_block_corruption_with_daemon`), but no production call site starts it when a user runs `ffs mount`. Corruption detection and RaptorQ recovery currently require explicit `ffs repair` / `ffs fsck --repair` invocation. Mount-path integration is tracked in the beads backlog.
+- **Mount background scrub is detection-only in V1.x.** `ffs mount` starts `ffs-repair::ScrubDaemon` automatically for default read-only mounts, owns cancellation through the mount lifecycle, and joins the worker on shutdown. Read-write mounts keep the daemon disabled by default; `--background-scrub` can opt into detection-only monitoring, `--no-background-scrub` disables the read-only default, and `--background-scrub-ledger` records evidence JSONL. RaptorQ symbol writes and block repair remain explicit `ffs repair` / `ffs fsck --repair` operations.
 - **External dependencies.** Workspace dependencies currently use crates.io releases (`asupersync = 0.2.5`, `ftui = 0.2.1`); local path overrides can be supplied with Cargo `[patch]` during sibling-repo development.
 - **Legacy reference corpus is not included.** The Linux kernel ext4/btrfs source used for behavioral extraction (~205K lines) is gitignored due to size. The extracted behavioral contracts are fully captured in [EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md](EXISTING_EXT4_BTRFS_STRUCTURE.md). For the original source, see `git.kernel.org/pub/scm/linux/kernel/git/torvalds/linux.git` at tag v6.19.
